required_documents: proof of funds ($10,000+ for first year)
common_mistakes: applying less than 4 months before start date

required_documents: proof of ties to home country
common_mistakes: applying without a valid underlying permit
trv.biometrics: Biometrics may be required based on nationality and last enrolment date.

pgwp.processing_time: up to 180 days (interim work authorization letter issued immediately on application)
pgwp.eligibility: Must apply within 180 days of receiving final marks or official graduation letter.
pgwp.duration: Matches length of study program, maximum 3 years
common_mistakes: not having attended an eligible DLI

sin.processing_time: Same day (in person at Service Canada)
sin.notes: Students and temporary workers receive a SIN beginning with 9. It expires when your permit expires. Update it when you get a new permit.

ohip.processing_time: 3-month waiting period from establishing Ontario residency
required_documents: proof of Ontario address (utility bill or bank statement)
ohip.notes: Apply at ServiceOntario. Some exemptions exist for shorter waiting period - check your permit type.
common_mistakes: not applying immediately on arrival (waiting period starts from application, not from arrival)

permit_extension.notes: Apply at least 30 days before expiry to maintain implied status. With implied status, you can continue under the same conditions while IRCC processes.

required_documents: proof of spouse status in Canada
marriage certificate or proof of common-law relationship
open_work_permit.notes: Common for spouses of international students and workers with eligible status.

required_documents: letter from school confirming mandatory co-op placement
coop_work_permit.notes: Required when work placement is mandatory component of studies.

flagpole.processing_time: Same day at border in some cases
flagpole.notes: Flagpoling is leaving and re-entering Canada via land border to activate a permit or status update. Border processing policies vary.

lmia.notes: LMIA is typically employer-led and supports employer-specific work permit applications.

definitions.TRV: Temporary Resident Visa - the stamp or sticker in your passport that allows you to enter Canada. Separate from study/work permits.
definitions.DLI: Designated Learning Institution - a school approved by a Canadian province or territory to host international students.
definitions.PGWP: Post-Graduation Work Permit - allows graduates of eligible Canadian schools to work in Canada after completing their studies.
definitions.eTA: Electronic Travel Authorization - required instead of a TRV for visa-exempt nationals flying into Canada.
definitions.IRCC: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada - the federal department that manages immigration, refugees, and citizenship.
definitions.flagpole: Informal term for briefly leaving Canada (often to a US border crossing) and re-entering to receive a new entry stamp or activate a newly issued permit.
definitions.implied status: If you apply to extend your permit before it expires, you may legally remain in Canada under the same conditions while IRCC processes your application.
definitions.LMIA: Labour Market Impact Assessment - a document from Employment and Social Development Canada that an employer may need before hiring a temporary foreign worker.
definitions.open work permit: A work permit that is not tied to a specific employer - the holder can work for almost any employer in Canada.
definitions.co-op work permit: A work permit required for mandatory work placements (co-op terms) as part of a study program. Usually approved alongside the study permit.
definitions.SIN: Social Insurance Number - a 9-digit number required to work in Canada and access government programs.
definitions.OHIP: Ontario Health Insurance Plan - the provincial health insurance program for residents of Ontario.
definitions.PR: Permanent Residence - the status of living in Canada permanently without being a citizen.
definitions.CRS: Comprehensive Ranking System - the points-based system used by Express Entry to rank candidates for permanent residence.
definitions.NOC: National Occupational Classification - Canada's system for classifying jobs and occupations, used in immigration applications.
definitions.biometrics: Fingerprints and photo collection required for many temporary resident applications.
definitions.AOR: Acknowledgement of Receipt - confirmation that IRCC has received your application.
definitions.GCKey: Secure government login system used to access IRCC online services.
definitions.study permit: A status document allowing international students to study in Canada under listed conditions.
definitions.work permit: A status document allowing temporary work in Canada under listed conditions.
definitions.visitor record: A document extending authorized stay in Canada without granting re-entry authorization.
definitions.primary applicant: The person who submits the principal immigration application for a family or group.
definitions.dependent: A spouse or child included under the primary applicant's file where applicable.

study_permit_expiry_soon.warning: Apply immediately - processing can take 60-150 days

trv_expiry_with_valid_permit.description: TRV expires but study/work permit is still valid
trv_expiry_with_valid_permit.warning: You cannot re-enter Canada without a valid TRV even if your permit is valid

graduation_approaching.warning: You must apply within 180 days of receiving final marks - do not delay

trv.missed: Your TRV expires but your study/work permit remains valid. You may stay in Canada but cannot re-enter if you travel internationally until you obtain a new TRV.
trv.action: Do not travel outside Canada until you have a new TRV.

study_permit.missed: You lose legal status in Canada and are required to leave or restore status immediately. Overstaying without implied status is a serious immigration violation.
study_permit.action: Contact IRCC immediately and do not leave Canada voluntarily without guidance.

pgwp.missed: You permanently lose eligibility to apply for the PGWP. The 180-day window cannot be extended. You would need to find an alternative pathway to work in Canada.
pgwp.action: Apply immediately — even a late application may be rejected.

work_permit.missed: You are no longer authorized to work. Continuing to work after expiry is a violation of your immigration conditions and may affect future applications.
work_permit.action: Cease work immediately and apply for renewal or a new permit.

permit_extension.missed: If you did not apply before expiry, you have no implied status and have lost legal authorization to remain in Canada.

study_permit.policy_note: As of November 8, 2024, eligible study permit holders may work up to 24 hours per week off campus during regular academic sessions. If a valid permit still says 20 hours, IRCC says the 24-hour rule still applies when the student is otherwise eligible.
